citation_and_locator: Yip, Ka-Fai. Syntax-prosody Mapping of Right Dislocation: A Comparative Study of Cantonese and Mandarin; ICPG-7 paper, pp. 15-16, ex. (44); related 2020 Phonological Externalization 5 paper
what_it_supports: Cantonese `jat1 go3 dou1 mou5 sik6` with a “haven't even eaten one” interpretation; directly attests a classifier minimizer with `dou1` and negative-perfective `mou5`.
limit: Example tests the sentence-particle boundary in right dislocation; it does not supply a minimizer theory, classifier inventory, or negator alternation.

citation_and_locator: Davis, Christopher, Zoe Pei-Sui Luk, and Grégoire Winterstein. 2024. Varieties of indefinites in Cantonese. Proceedings of Sinn und Bedeutung 28:254-271; pp. 259-260, ex. (16) and table 2
what_it_supports: Plain `[CL N]` and `[jat1 CL N]` under `mou5` lack the low-scope any reading in the reported contrast, unlike a bare noun; establishes the ordinary-indefinite scope boundary.
limit: Does not analyze `jat1 CL dou1` minimizers; judgments are based on one native Hong Kong Cantonese author and require broader replication.

citation_and_locator: Basciano, Bianca. 2015. Creatività bilingue e contatto linguistico nella pubblicità cinese. In Abbiati and Greselin (eds.), La lingua cinese: variazioni sul tema, pp. 147-172; p. 167, ex. (11), checking Lister 2009 against the original television advertisement
what_it_supports: Natural Hong Kong advertising attestation `Special Moment jat1 go3 dou1 m4 wui5 Miss`, translated as not missing a single special moment; verifies an `m4`-containing count-minimizer surface.
limit: Code-mixed advertisement and contextual head omission; not a controlled syntax, scope, or productivity study.

citation_and_locator: CantoDict. `一啲 jat1 di1`; examples `一啲都唔熱嘅`, `一啲都唔易`, and `一啲都唔型`
what_it_supports: Marks the form as Cantonese and explicitly describes `jat1 di1 dou1 m4 + adjective` as emphatic negation meaning not at all.
limit: Community dictionary examples do not establish a formal analysis or merger with count/classifier minimizers.

citation_and_locator: Law, Yan Kei Ann. 2004. Sentence-final focus particles in Cantonese. PhD dissertation, University College London; chapter 5
what_it_supports: Scalar-focus context involving Cantonese `dou1`, `lin4`, and sentence-final particles.
limit: Does not directly establish the negative minimal-quantity construction or its negator/classifier boundaries.
